ascents.comment: first 9
wacky sharp and painful
jump for style points
special boulder, world class
campus beta
hard start god damn
cut feet in every move for the actual difficulty
feels tall, don't break an ankle
if you put a knee pad on for this climb you’re soft
don't use pads, they get in the way of dabbin
pre-break
ugly send
soaking wet, pouring rain... don't do it when wet
don't stack more than 1 pad
ugliest flash ever
sooooo good
just jump to the jug, gaston beta stupid
definition of hal climb
first ten! taught myself to toehook for a year just to send this
fun moves, normal left line looks choss
like velcro in 30 F
clunk
drop knee foot jam easiest beta, get flexy
wow i dyno
contrived but sick moves
definition of points chasing
one of the coolest climbs i’ve had the pleasure of working out. horrible name.
ass
easier than alexisizer in kraft red rock

ascents.zlaggableName: Played Like A Poop Butt Sit

ascents.comment: span
so much easier when you don’t cut feet on every other move
mini nemesis, needed temps
never have i ever hated the process of working a climb so much but enjoyed the send go
mantle took about 2 mins to get sprayed down
soft
made no progress across 5 sessions, then sent with best crew (shoutout fiona)
punted 2 times at the lip, but sweaty hands were defeated
kinda choss but super fun
thought i was going to die, my spotter was kinda sus, but we made it
there’s too many ways to top this out and they’re all ez but droppable
all bout boxes
only when it's baking in the sun does it feel 7a
knocked the shin on the flash, tragic
gym climb
would be 5 stars if it was a pure fall
nemisis
spooky bad feet
barefoot second go
worst climb ever
use the big foot for the first move, not the smear
pinky claw grip is goated
good
perfect razors
hard long and spicy
lip is sooner than you think
would be 5 stars if not for the power lines above it, otherwise such a sick line
soft
contender for most awful
glassy. not best in america by a LONGSHOT. hunk 3x better at least.
such sick features, just wish it was harder/longer
mantel scary, but v ez
grovel and grind
fuck the toehook
beta i used prob just hard, felt way harder than scrawny/flu
squammy shufflin
dragged 10 people across the freezing river for 2 attempts, best decision of my life. perfect line.
scary mantel, gotta place the right foot higher than you think
soaking wet jug, made the send more rewarding
v6, horrible downclimb, so cold
first 8a, double dyno
right heel start, huck n swing
jump n pray
close high feet
put a fan on the right hand pinch edge, #condies
meh
if it wasnt in a swamp it'd be so good
did the last move a lil spicy & fast
super fun, mega shoulders on good crimps. punted the mantel a few days before, but came back with the day flash
looked at it in october in a lil drizzle, said i can flash. came back in a lil drizzle=ez #manifest
campused a LOT of it
i did, in fact have many troublems… biffed the top too many times, lower portion is ez and soft. double jam and wiggle beach whale ftw 🐋
tried quite hard
overrated
easy rookie vibes. more gym bro than crimper
took 5:30 to top out. slab crux. double clutch dyno is the way
v fun, slick feet
lowest part of the right hand start hold was essential
have you done this this in crocs blind folded tho...?
higher line of crimps is better line and honestly just as easy as the lower line
too dabby
imo harder than all the rest squam 7s except baba but tbh baba should prob get the upgrade
harder than dr. bigglesworth by at least a half or full grade.. but dr is soooooft
tried it barefoot, was scary, second try with shoes on, i guess im not built like charles
very leavy. a harder more awkward version of pimpsqueak. horrible approach. the intro left arete match was fun, the rest of the slapping was mid. pro tip: look at the final right hand to slap it.
ouch, but yum
sick techy lip traverse finish,  hitting the jug feels oh so crispy. soft squamish style.
ouch my wrist. high right foot first. d luo beta goated
perfect balancy start, squeezing hard into the stand, flows better than the merced.
don’t get baited by the middle rail sloper. go up for the highest gaston.
sick
just stand up. my wrists hurt. sent after a brutal punt foot slip. got way too pumped on those jugs, lowkey almost died but held it together.
soft. choss.
what a sick boulder. did not climb it perfect but got to enjoy the board-esque moves and what a top out. pure joy. tape the ring finger for the start left hand so you can gaston like a mofo
much more engaging than mori, but less flowy. still great
skinned my shin trying to cheat it, def goes, way harder. did the sit n spin.
unreal line, terrible choss too put, horrible approach. glad i did it.
didnt do the funky cross, just smacked the crimp, hiked the feet and hucked. consolation for the fucked up furry pocket killing my vibe.
holy shit this thing is good. balancey, a lil sketchy, comfy holds. might be the most fun i've had on a 7b in while. loses a star for the landing/lose af starting hold/low holds.
lowkey the mantel felt like the crux...
lovely left crimp cut my tip open had to do with tape. thank god my index finger isn't that useful anyways. if it wasn't for the first move it'd be 5 stars fs.
cut feet lots, classic hal beta.
glassy but great moves
weeeee
pinkyless ascent. lock off on the heel. so sandy, but surprisingly bomber (for now?)
sick heel tension

perfect non-left pinky climb, scary pull on but actually quite chill. took so many attempts to link the bump without using the pinky on the lip, but landed it perfectly to take it to the chossy ass topout. kinda shitty lowball and awful approach but i had a good time.
fuck me the first hold is sharp. otherwise super fun, lots of enjoyable moves, mantled out right, kinda love the mono. overshadowed by everything else in the area, but a worthy climb to check out
don’t top out right. easy when you know the beta. first try in 4 years. way too many pads needed for this, d luo with the beta farming.
kinda scary solo, bit licheny at the top out but really fun lil boulder
doesn’t really feel like anything in squamish but ok

kinda hard to grade so going with others, 7C sounds reasonable. wasn’t too crazy of a dyno but the establish and arm pogo hop was pretty fun to work out. two dynos in one climb?! sick one on a rest day, no warm up needed.

there are boulders that always stick out, whether a grade breakthrough, life circumstance, purest of lines, climbing movement, setting, or the people you share it with. my left pinky post pulley pop felt just barely ready enough to pull the crux move, and it's been a while since i've executed well on something hard for me while solo. the pad hauling trips (over TWO different days) alone are enough to certify the effort i put into this one. i haven't been this ecstatic pulling the lip on a climb in a while. my heart was racing and my smile was beaming. can't recommend this one enough. so sick.
thuggy and short:) topout was toasty af but somehow found the right slopers to pull myself over the lip. cut move was siiiiiick. also saw a cute lil blue tailed snake.
two moves, fun.
fuck me that right hand crimp hurts ow. dope jump tho
perfect board style climb. sick starting heel and the dropknees were so fun. such a shame it's a drop off
nearly biffed the catch, and absolutely shredded my forearm catching this thing. felt a bit cooked on the send go, but nice one for a first double-double. mid start next!
felt kinda desperate but not like physically limiting, was weird. jammed my right heel on top of my ring finger real hard and had to wiggle it out, that was the crux fs. such fun moves!
wicked fun crimps n edges. only complaint is the lichen on the slab and the sharpness of the lip crimp. vert 7a at it's best
7A? hard? maybe. kinda sick two mover. wish i had the beans for the sit, one day...

probably the most i've held my breath. such sick body tension and rock up and over move. did some sick hand jamming to top the whole thing out, #crackisback. it's insane how chossy the whole rock is but how bomber the holds are.
really fun! feels more even move-by-move than giving tree. nice to crimp on that boulder.
very flowy. tiny pad graze dab on the jugs, oh well. no standout crux, just builds on nice comfy moves.
quite comfy holds, good line, had to move fast to not get pumped.

im taking the 11 cause i ain't a tall boi. softy, definitely. but fun two mover. set up all my pads and got absolutely THRASHED by a colorado hailstorm. had to take a 2 hour break, and fan/jet fan so much rock to get it back to climbable shape. setting is so pretty:)
ngl that last right hand sloper was really hard to hold. maybe it was skin, maybe it was cause i had tape, but i had to fight a lil. heel rock over felt so fun!
im taking the flash even tho the first and last moves are the same of the kind. that right hand is so fun, and the lock offs are really really good. shame its contrived af.
underclings were kinda hard, but the crimp moves at the top and spicy mantle brought the quality up.

can't span like the tall boys, suck them hips in and low and short beta! pysched to come back for the low this thing was so fun, but jesus someone needs to jackhammer those two dab rocks away i was scared
simple cozy kilter v6 with outdoor edges. pretty clean obvious line, very flowy and what a view on the top god damn. dabbed on a foot cut ;(
long. i am not a sport climber.
3rd go, shoulda flashed, just chose wrong top beta. so fun
top out crux fs
tick marks were the crux. pysched for the low
so hard
fun!
lowkey kinda fire
car got broken into and it was pouring
this mantle sucks
good fun

3rd go after not committing on the big move and really butchering my beta. harder than bierstadt but what does that mean? surprisingly fun moves and holds, fits my fingers nicely. i love the micro right hand crimp before the weird holds, so yum.

approach through the talus was not the move. got my ass handed to me after topping this, truly had to fight at the lip. felt out of body and lucid for 10 mins after walking down, i think i'm not built for long boulders at altitude. i think i like it a lot more in retrospect but i was lowkey dying during the sesh. s/o to hannah for bringing my shoes down after i forgot them in the cave.
biffed the mantel cause i was too high and right. got so cooked from the punt, but thankfully if you mantel a tad bit left it's so easy. feels heroic and well set. good good
comfy holds, cool moves! fun! best hole i've crawled out of so far in rmnp. if only the sit was a tad bit more comfy... (i split even through the tape!)
got sprayed and prayed. low holds are super fun. the lip was not as good as i expected. sketchy downclimb options.
god, i cannot recover on jugs at all. the pump goes crazy. roof was fun and chill tho.
softy jugs.
so fun!

slipped off the top after mantling 3/4 of the way, thought i was gonna die. chossiest, dirtiest, glassiest direct finish with no holds. but i traved right and did it next go, still thought i was gonna die, thank god for tinyman spotting me.
big fun moves, amazing topout. wish the slide rock wasn't there, what a mega boulder it would be.
my footwork is improving! at least on this climb, not skipper d. stiff left shoe and some grit. fun fun fun!
ascents.zlaggableName: Far Left El Jorge / The Core

ascents.comment: there's nothing like a humbling climb that shows how bad at climbing i am. got the mega spray, and tbh the only reason it didn't help was me. i am bad at using feet. thankfully redeemed myself on core. and the top out was smooth like buttah.
small box? like crimps? great feet? big jump? yes please! glad i got to do try this one, yum yum yum.

linear progression from flash attempt to send. 5? ish? attempts, kept inching move by move. cleaned up beta, and felt the flow. fan was essential to keep the skeeters at bay. great night! kinda like a perfect board climb that just keeps going and going.
toe hookin goodness! psyched for the low! got a great spray down, just had to learn to actually use my hips and legs and it went ez, fun and so so comfy

after two insanely heartbreaking punts two nights prior, i mustered the courage to come back. and punt again. however! i found the comb and perfectly parted the hair down to the last strand and fought to stick the horrendous little crimp on the lip. i was not alone in the house of punting, as sheldon pulled a hal, however, he the wiser person found better beta for next time. astounding how hard 1 move and a few bumps makes the stand so much harder. i like this climb.
ascents.zlaggableName: I comb my hair like god

ascents.comment: finally! been through the ringer with this one. was raining while i was warming up, and just kept drizzling til my send go. thank god the rock didnt get soaked, cause i was gripped on the topout. for how uncomfy and sharp the holds are, its such a good line and surprisingly comfy. vert techy crimps! :)
good early exit, fun very precise lil crimps. yummy! kinda an awkward topout campus, but happy for the chiller walk to the top.
scary scary. my right hand was so pumped i couldn't close it on the jug. thankful to check out boulders like this one, spooky, powerful, and flowy. underrated for sure.
god dammit i biffed the flash. good comfy, lil spooky with the rain coming down, but the spray was good. if you're the right height and burly, suits ya good. softie?

so fun, delicate, techy. couldn't power my way through, just had to sink deeper into the toes. was fighting thin skin for sure, thankful for fans (and liquid chalk). such a pretty setting, absolutely peak bouldering.
split a tip right as i was making progress on cbs. consolation send, but actually a very good climb. inside flag goodness
funky! got the best beta from mer and marci, lovely last day in rmnp with some lovely people. still need to work on my footwork tho...
called it soft. came back and punted cause i was trying to steez and campus the final move. kept punting. had to come back over a year later and use my feet, still soft tho.

3 sessions...... harder than tatonka, bee prof, worm world cave, secret lives, etc FOR SURE. god what an insane line, bless who ever built the landing, may all your holds be crisp. oblivion is so good guys don't come here.
kapow! i'm a comp kid today. send train with scott on this one AND bloodwyrm.
i'm taking 10 at 5'8". but its a softie, for sure. caught the jug with 2 fingers, friction was good in that 91F heat.
scary committing move to the false lip.
last of the squam v8 dynos! 91F aint got shit on me.
good pinch! love me a tight box heel hook.
awkward but kinda fun, barely hit the jug, almost fell.
50th double digit! yahooo! also this is soft af. basically 2 moves. not dabbing was chill, just go slow. maybe one day ill have the balls to top this out
came here in between the rain. punted. got sad. listened to careless talk, laughed. was fighting the right hand sloper, took a sec, locked tf in and barely got er done. such a lovely rock.
learned to use my hips and feet.
blew the flash cause i didn't look at the topout. top out crux fr. hawt day

probably the least pleasant first move on an otherwise five star perfect line. got the crew up there after leaving michael clocked in at sakura mart, fueled by onigiri, plopped down the 13 pads and ripped it first go from the bottom. felt so flowy and amazing, cherished the company and the oblivon vibes. always a good day outside in squamish. s/o aron for lending the foam 3/4 sessions.
finally got er done. nemesis fr
sqaumish's most mid classic

2nd ascent? banger! bit gritty, but the moves are wicked fun, tiger did good with this one. that right hand crimp is so fricken bad, and i thought it was just insanely hard, but on the send go, really didn't feel too bad, so V10 seems pretty fair. glad obie was there, woulda definitely downclimbed or shit my pants without his pad shifting and spotting. very very fun! i did lots of blowing pine needles away on my top out lol, maybe rope it up...
lap #32

i am bad at finishing climbs. moves are easy, sending is hard. is it soft? probably? can i actually execute? once. drizzle came down the canyon, i had erased ticks/half packed up, and decided to wait it out. didn't get that wet and i split on the last right hand on the send go. finally done with this thing, at least it wasn't my first 8a so i held myself to that promise.
its alllllll in the legs. took a few beta refinements down low, and had 2 tips fully taped up. very fun! good leavy rock.

floaty dyno! had an epic on session one, but lowkey just sandbagging myself. amazing what a difference putting the right foot first on versus the left. yikessss im bad. oh well. glad they cleaned and had the vision, really really enjoyable top sequence! (ignore all the choss in between the holds lol)
got sprayed down a little tooooooooo much. people need to be quiet more often lol
good grips, fun hand jammy top out
hard! kinda desperate on the topout.
not my finest moment.
very fun! got to send train with d luo and p! her first 6!
powerrrrr
got SO pumped
comfy comfy slopey
kinda sharp on the right index... sad i had to be taped and couldn't finish portabello
physical. forearm was losing it's shit, had to beach whale. never had that much pain in the flexor.
mightve fucked the beta? oh well
3rd go. drop knee heaven.
oh, my, lordy. such a good one. perfectly in style, comfy, clean/pure. happy to fly across the country for this one. 😍

felt insanely tweaky on almost every hard move until i found the flow. softy? yes. v9? probably. did i put in more work than instinct or any other 7c ive done recently? yes. i take those. so so so good. incredibly satisfying, did not feel the fear, only the flow. thanks to the NC crew for the foam and stoke. #blessedbe
sooooo fun, only wish it went on forever
comfy comfy comfy. only downside is the start position and the chosspile of a hill you top out on. gud gud
crux was the pain my left hand endured holding those slopers. send train with jenkypoo followed by dannypoo shortly after. heel toe cam rose was sick.
i was so cold. not actually that sharp. sit will have to wait for a day i’m not 3 days on.
got so pumped on the topout. had to take a sec, feels nice to finally do something in the valley, its been a rough fall lol
wahoo! didn't punt this time! theo said i was on 1.5x speed, it when im at my best.
edges n crimps with a spicy topout (but actually its just a flat ok landing). just had to climb fast and snatchy with a little try hard
flowy, except the topout. comfy comfy. almost numbed tf out. pretty boulder.
lowkey got spanked...
sad to not flash. didn't wanna lose skin, so limited burns, but then dluo was lowkey epicing. popped the boots on and walked it. vic's beta is goated (thumbpress to get the right foot up)

two taped tips, splits galore. pretty tweaky undercling. all this aside it’s a pretty great boulder. sad i couldn’t flash (missed the opportunity for a dluo bevy), maybe next reincarnation. mantle did NOT feel secure, but we ball.
so much better than squirrel... havent done it tho. so fun, tall-ish, spicy-ish, hard-ish. pretty much my ideal 7A. high heel felt steezy (thx to friendly person for beta)
pretty full value climb. bit gritty with tape, but a fun one to cap off my valley season
the pinch is nice 🧱
kinda just an easier free willy. which means it’s a preeeetty fun one. yoreo up next
unfortunate foot slip… low point of the day
shoutout to hannah for being a roley poley. just needed to not be in agonizing pain due to existence. lowkey topout was the highlight.
kindof an awesome roof with sick moves and comfy holds. such a shame it’s only 2 feet tall
topout was an absolute choss pile. but the roof was primo 💰
soft seconds goes. first right hand move then sent it to the hueco. the other holds are fake news 😉 thanks for the guide encouraging me to be a steezy boi
footwork masterclass. alison’s statement on my climbing: embarrassing
bring ya beans
ascents.zlaggableName: Alf in a Blender / Hunger Artist

ascents.comment: almost makes me think i’m a good toehooker…
should have flashed. had bad top put beta. but, as alison said, since i didn’t flash i got to climb it twice. fav of hueco this year. wish it just went on forever 🥲
probably the hardest style of board crimping for me. split a tip and grunted real loud to bag this one
kinda unpleasant start holds but logical and easy. just another excuse to do new religion:)
p yummy holds, lovely little romp. heel toe cam start
classic
can’t believe my foot stayed… one move wonder but there’s no wonder that it’s a bad climb
kinda shit myself on the top out…. fought for my life on those chossy ratters, but didn’t die. thanks for the encouragement crew!
lowkey kinda hard pulling around the lip to the jug… but very good roof moves to get there

bit of a wild ride on this one. direct finish, through the heucos, the way god intended. took an absolute digger slightly off the pads and into the tree a bit (thankfully no injury), thanks kasra big time for saving my ass. got through to the huecos 3 times and fell each time and thought i was done for, but just as the day was winding down i snuck it in, to the top with a little heart attack moment before the lip. big thanks yoreo + crew for the support and 18+ pads/spots. heart is happy and full
kinda infuriating trying to learn the toehook. sent by turning off the 🧠 and just snatching
knee pads, so obviously soft. hero move to hueco so much more satisfying from below
honestly, doesn't climb all that well... might be the worst part of dark age...
i still hate 3 finger drag. bruised cheeks and spite got me up this
i hate hate hate hate being injured. 3 finger drag will be my downfall.

FUCK YOU 3 FINGER DRAG
had to do it twice and surgery on my instinct heel for the second lap
shoulda woulda coulda flashed. cold cold pumped hands. p sick
be a good samaritan and help brush ticks
i come to you, yet again to say, i am bad at finishing climbs. maybe dial in a good beta before rips from the bottom...? naaaaaah
pro: i can do the entire climb and punt 10 times and still have margin. con: i am stupid. just yomp and don't use your feet hal, its obvious. (harder than white horse fs)
my three finger drag might be improving... good bloc, bad hike. i hate cholla.
sheeeeeeeesh, can't crimp BUT i can triple paddle... fucking sick!
sloppy slaps
non egregious dab on send go #1, no dab 15 mins later. difficulty is all in the right hand thumb placement. palm press to hold feels sick, but the dab factor is ass.
desperate af. harder than every other 9 in rr, and harder than rtwh fer shur. fricken nails crux and lowkey the top moves from the bottom a bit sus. line is a beaut.
i can't make v4 look pretty
big 3 finger drag W
got spit out and turned around, a good indicator it's time to take some rest days
if this was set in a gym it would be the worst piece of plastic ever drilled to plywood... but since it's on rock its fire

solo mission up the hill. what a squeezer! even though i'm detracting stars for the hollowness of the jugs it's still world class. wish i had the composure to make that topout smooth. downclimb jump crux.
made the top a lil too spicy than necessary

fuck you 3 finger drag! just when i thought i couldn’t punt further than i had before i did it again… but i got that shit on lock now. big ups to andy loaning the foam 👍🏽
ps. i miss crimping so much
wow, best 9 in bishop??? absolutely perfect grips, lovely setting, and movement! crux is the river crossing/kitty litter hillside. fantastic!
yippeeeee
comfy little yomp, started the 🚂 for the boys
almost biffed… the low looks like shit, but this was kinda fun. pockets are good 👍🏽
crocs + palm press
felt hard til i figured my way. hips! very fun!
kinda only two moves... but the pump from pterodactyl low got me, nearly dropped the vB top. unexpectedly very fun! low seems 2 grades harder... but maybe it/s 7b+...?
toe jam was fun
best sandcastle i ever did climb. wish i could give it 5 stars but the constant darude-sandstorm really isn’t ideal
crocs, 5’8” +2, 150lbs, southeast asian male, late 20s, mullet, steezy af
heel toe cam! right! (left and center are stupid)
ascents.sectorName: Cabin Fever / The Perimeter / The Death Scream Area

ascents.comment: the old man groans
hard af at my span… the top lip traverse is sick tho
toe hook the lip!
first two moves are ass, but the rest is primo. good first alpine day. so nice being back in colorado where all the 10s are soft #egolifting
weeeee spooky but fun
so fun!
pogo!
almost flashed, then had an epic. i am dumb.
shiiiiit shoulda tried a little harder on the flash. the mini pinch is yummy, what a nice little romp
that first move is a lot. taking the full points cause it’s my bday 🥳
spooky but really fun!
toe hook? heel hook? dropknee?? footwork is very challenging.
kneebar intro (idgaf bout fa, its obvious). very fun moves! feels easier than habilis tbh... 🤷🏾‍♂️ so who knows what grade, CO is soft.
not hard but not free. gotta really try. funky moves! good trainer to be actively thinking about all ya limbs!
flowy!
big time brain farts on this one
short beta with high heel toe cam and the glassy pinch. sick! gotta choke up and squeeze!
an epic battle in sunshine and sweaty tips (btb w/ peter). TWO makitas+clouds! could be 7b? or 7c+?? (a philosophical debate of experience vs the send)
must be really nice to feel the flow on this... but i was not warm and kinda lost
70F and sunny wasn't ideal... feels harder than out the cut fs, so 🤷🏾‍♂️. bottom beta was so fun, big kneebar tech! chipped chosspile climb surprisingly well...
p nails w/o bearing down on the left hand. harder than tail lol
gym climb
aint no way free basin and this are the same grade... (hint, don't use the higher left crimp)
very techy low crux! aesthetic af, happy to have made the trek
holds suited me so well! great night out with kyle & shelly. freaked em out a lil with my topout but i felt great 😅. absolute stunner!
left start. imagine making the gem that is tRolls, and just enshitifying it. shouldn't have chased the points and just done the stand for most fun.
kinda just being a dumbass
ascents.zlaggableName: Le Toit du Cul de Chien

ascents.comment: holy shit this is so good
very good, just subtle enough to be interesting
dyno to undercling catch. not short friendly or heat friendly
spicy
the top was HAWT
so sandy, but sick line
jammy
sloper was hot-n-slimy. the falls are wild
banger. absolute piss at my height
somehow no dab with the campus come in
bap bap!
morpho af. soft 173cm, ya boy got hops
kinda sick, should be a classique
ascents.zlaggableName: La Pierre et le Sabre / La Belle Arête

ascents.comment: bit reachy
hot and sweaty. every sloper was baking in the 30/87 heat. very fun!
slick n hard
oops, did it as an sds, lowkey felt 7a+ that way... but wowza the top is yum!
bit wet today... big cheezin tho
womp!
first go from the bottom after yesterdays 30 minute rapid fire sesh of racing against the rain. very yummy compression! loved the beta, felt so flowy
sooo fun!
wet wet wet! avoid the wet!
once i found my beta it was cheeeez. good crimping!
sweet grips, spooky fall
minor dabbage but we got boulders to go do #staybusy
campus tag in! sendy 🚂 w/ cadance! such delish grips
sick, kinda hard for 6c+ ngl
in fact there might be a spoon...? what a great experience! beautiful as can be!
mega comfy
soooo good! my little taste of board climbing by the river 🥰, def worth topping out to actually top the boulder!
barefoot! great crew, insane scenery. in love with this valley.

waited 2 hours for the window on this one after getting mega inspired by cadance. shoulda flashed tbh, but so goes slopers when wet. just blessed to have not been struck by lightning. maybe 7c+ whilst raining and 90% humidity in june.
